title: Complete the implementation

content.problemStatement: Given a selectionSort function that accepts an array as a parameter, sort the array using the **Selection sort** algorithm.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case consists of two lines: the first line contains an integer N, the size of the array. The second line contains N space‑separated integers.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output the array sorted in non‑decreasing order on a single line. Numbers are separated by a single space.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The array is repeatedly scanned to find the smallest remaining element and swapped to the front, producing a sorted sequence.
Each pass moves the smallest unsorted element to its correct position, turning the descending order into ascending order.
The input is already sorted; selection sort performs swaps that leave the array unchanged.

solution.approach: Selection sort repeatedly selects the smallest element from the unsorted portion of the array and swaps it with the first unsorted element. By scanning the remaining part of the array to find the minimum index for each position, the array becomes sorted after N‑1 passes. The algorithm is simple to implement and works in‑place, requiring only constant extra space. Although its time complexity is O(N^2), the given constraints (N ≤ 1000) make it fast enough for the problem.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N^2) per test case, O(T·N^2) overall.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) auxiliary space besides the input array.

code.Java: import java.io.*;import java.util.*;public class Main{private static void selectionSort(int[] arr){int n=arr.length;for(int i=0;i<n-1;i++){int minIdx=i;for(int j=i+1;j<n;j++){if(arr[j]<arr[minIdx]){minIdx=j;}}int tmp=arr[i];arr[i]=arr[minIdx];arr[minIdx]=tmp;}}public static void main(String[] args)throws Exception{BufferedReader br=new BufferedReader(new InputStreamReader(System.in));StringBuilder out=new StringBuilder();int T=Integer.parseInt(br.readLine().trim());while(T-- >0){int N=Integer.parseInt(br.readLine().trim());int[] arr=new int[N];StringTokenizer st=new StringTokenizer(br.readLine());for(int i=0;i<N;i++){arr[i]=Integer.parseInt(st.nextToken());}selectionSort(arr);for(int i=0;i<N;i++){out.append(arr[i]);if(i+1<N)out.append(' ');}out.append('\n');}System.out.print(out.toString());}}
